about.subtitle: Ph.D. Candidate at HKUST NLP Group
about.bio_text:
I am a second-year Ph.D. candidate at [HKUST NLP Group](https://github.com/hkust-nlp), advised by Professor [Junxian He](https://jxhe.github.io/). I received my B.Eng. from [Shanghai Jiao Tong University (SJTU)](https://en.sjtu.edu.cn/) in 2024.

My research focuses on natural language processing and machine learning, with an emphasis on:

- Agentic LLMs and long-horizon reasoning
- Logical reasoning and reinforcement learning
- Vision-language models and perception bottlenecks
- LLM truthfulness and interpretability

Before starting my Ph.D., I worked as a research intern at Tencent WXG and Shanghai AI Lab. I was a research intern at MiniMax from February 2025 to February 2026, contributing to the MiniMax M1 and M2.x models.

I am broadly interested in building trustworthy and capable reasoning systems.

news.body_markdown: Our paper **[WebExplorer](https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.06501)** was released on arXiv for long-horizon web-agent training.
Our paper **[On the Perception Bottleneck of VLMs for Chart Understanding](https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.18435)** was accepted to **EMNLP 2025 Findings**.
Our paper **[On the Universal Truthfulness Hyperplane Inside LLMs](https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.08582)** was presented at **EMNLP 2024**.
Joined **HKUST NLP Group** as a Ph.D. student advised by Prof. Junxian He.

cv.name: Junteng Liu
cv.label: Ph.D. Candidate
cv.summary: Ph.D. candidate at HKUST NLP Group working on reasoning-capable and trustworthy language models.

education.institution: Hong Kong University of Science and Technology

highlights: Worked on large-scale logical reasoning data synthesis and training.
Studied perception bottlenecks in vision-language models for chart understanding.
Worked on LLM truthfulness and internal representation analysis.
